---
title: "1.2 Variables and Data Types"
subtitle: "Part 3: Weather and Mad Libs Challenges"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit1-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Debugging Challenge

### Weather Report

Debug the following code that reads out a weather report.

Make sure the data types match the values put into the variables.

Can you find all the bugs and get the code to run?

Work with a programming buddy if you get stuck.

## Code Window

### `activecode:: challenge1-2-weather`

```java
public class Challenge1_2_weather
{
   public static void main(String[] args)
   {
       int temperature = 70.5;
       double tvChannel = 101;
       boolean sunny = 1

       System.out.print("Welcome to the weather report on Channel ")
       System.out.println(TVchannel);
       System.out.print("The temperature today is );
       System.out.println(tempurature);
       System.out.print("Is it sunny today? ");
       System.out.println(sunny);
   }
}
```

## Test Requirements

### `activecode:: challenge1-2-weather`

Runestone expects this output from `main`:

```text
Welcome to the weather report on Channel 101
The temperature today is 70.5
Is it sunny today? true
```

## Debugging Focus

### `activecode:: challenge1-2-weather`

::: {.tight-list}
- `temperature` must use a data type that can store `70.5`.
- `tvChannel` should print as `101`, not `101.0`.
- `sunny` must be a `boolean` value.
- Statements need semicolons.
- String literals need closing quotes.
- Variable names must match spelling and capitalization exactly.
:::

## Groupwork Coding Challenge

### Mad Libs

Have you ever played MAD LIBS?

In this game, you first choose a bunch of words following clues like give me a color or a plural noun, without looking at the story.

Then those words are filled into the story to make it sound very wacky.

Fill in the variables below with silly words, and then run to see the wacky story.

## Pair Task

### `activecode:: challengeMadLibs`

Working in pairs, come up with another silly story that uses at least <span class="mark">5 new `String` variables</span>.

When you are done, try another team's mad libs code.

Your teacher may ask you to create this program in a Java IDE that can do input, for example with `Scanner`.

External IDE and `Scanner` directions are optional; the classroom slide keeps the static starter task.

## Code Window

### `activecode:: challengeMadLibs`

```java
public class MadLibs1
{
    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        // fill these in with silly words/strings (don't read the poem yet)
        String pluralnoun1 = "Replace";
        String color1 = "Replace";
        String color2 = "Replace";
        String food = "Replace";
        String pluralnoun2 = "Replace";

        // Run to see the silly poem!
        System.out.println("Roses are " + color1);
        System.out.println(pluralnoun1 + " are " + color2);
        System.out.println("I like " + food);
        System.out.println("Do " + pluralnoun2 + " like them too?");

        // Now come up with your own silly poem!
    }
}
```

## Test Requirements

### `activecode:: challengeMadLibs`

Runestone expects the poem shape:

```text
Roses are *
* are *
I like *
Do * like them too?
```

It also checks that all `"Replace"` values have been changed.

## Completion Tasks

### `activecode:: challengeMadLibs`

::: {.tight-list}
- Replace every `"Replace"` string with a word that matches the variable name.
- Keep variables outside quotes when printing their values.
- Preserve the four starter poem output lines.
- Create your own silly story using at least 5 new `String` variables.
- Use meaningful names for new variables.
:::

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

::: {.tight-list}
- debug type mismatches such as assigning `70.5` to `int`
- debug `boolean` values using `true` or `false`
- fix missing semicolons and missing string quotes
- keep variable spelling and capitalization consistent
- replace placeholder `String` values in Mad Libs
- add at least five new `String` variables for the paired story
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
